hypothesis: If open-source molecular generative models are pre-trained on large chemical datasets, fine-tuned on a curated CTF dataset, and optimized with reinforcement learning using expert-crafted rewards (flatness, symmetry, conjugation, steric hindrance, SMARTS patterns), then they will generate a higher fraction of valid, unique, novel, and expert-filter-passing CTF candidates with improved FCD and diversity than the same models without one or more of these knowledge-transfer steps because hierarchical transfer and reward shaping embed task-relevant structural priors that guide generation toward desired porous carbon motifs.
